page: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation with 4 physical cores and single-thread-per-core execution. The processor features a 32 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Storage is bundled with a local 800 GB NVMe SSD, providing high-capacity local storage. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and memory read bandwidth fall within the average range. However, write bandwidth for large blocks and single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show strong performance, ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-threaded decompression and database operations perform in the lower 25%, indicating potential bottlenecks under highly parallelized disk or database-heavy workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high memory and storage density relative to its 4-vCPU compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff, as resource contention may affect sustained multi-core performance. This hardware profile is well-suited for memory-cached applications, Redis key-value stores, lightweight data processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring moderate compute combined with substantial memory and local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. This shared-CPU instance includes a bundled 800 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, alongside strong large-block memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-threaded decompression and database operations show weaker relative performance. With an 8.0 GB per core memory allocation, this server provides a high-density memory profile at a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. It is best suited for memory-cached applications, Redis key-value stores, and general-purpose workloads requiring substantial memory and local storage.

32.0 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio
